parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ filterGroups: [{ filters: [{ propertyName: 'createdate', operator: 'GTE', value: String(new Date(Date.UTC(new Date().getUTCFullYear(), new Date().getUTCMonth(), new Date().getUTCDate() - 1)).getTime()) }, { propertyName: 'createdate', operator: 'LT', value: String(new Date(Date.UTC(new Date().getUTCFullYear(), new Date().getUTCMonth(), new Date().getUTCDate())).getTime()) }] }], properties: ['name', 'domain', 'linkedin_company_page', 'numberofemployees', 'group_number_of_employees'], limit: 200, ...($json.after ? { after: $json.after } : {}) }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
// See prompts/prompt-classify-batch.md for full prompt documentation
const items = $input.all();
const companies = items.map((item, index) => ({
  index,
  name: item.json.companyName,
  domain: item.json.domain
}));

const prompt = 'You are an expert at identifying corporate subsidiaries and group companies.\n\n'
  + 'For each company below, determine if it is:\n'
  + '- INDEPENDENT (standalone business)\n'
  + '- SUBSIDIARY/BRANCH of a larger parent company or group\n\n'
  + 'Classify as SUBSIDIARY only when you have strong evidence. Return a confidence level:\n\n'
  + 'HIGH CONFIDENCE — unambiguous, well-known relationships:\n\n'
  + '1. WELL-KNOWN GLOBAL FIRM + EXPLICIT GEOGRAPHY/REGION in the name:\n'
  + '   - "KPMG Spain" -> KPMG, "Deloitte Netherlands" -> Deloitte, "PwC Austria" -> PwC\n'
  + '   - The parent is the same brand without the geography\n'
  + '   - Must be a universally recognized global brand (Big 4, MBB, global law firms, etc.)\n'
  + '   - But "KPMG" alone (no geography) -> INDEPENDENT (could be the parent itself)\n\n'
  + '2. SAME BRAND + OPERATIONAL SUFFIX in the name:\n'
  + '   - "Weber-Stephen Products EMEA" -> Weber-Stephen Products\n'
  + '   - "Jones Bros Civil Engineering UK" -> Jones Bros Civil Engineering\n'
  + '   - The base name is identical, only a region/division word is added\n\n'
  + '3. UNIVERSALLY KNOWN OWNERSHIP — relationships that are common public knowledge:\n'
  + '   - Tech: Instagram -> Meta, YouTube -> Alphabet, WhatsApp -> Meta, LinkedIn -> Microsoft, GitHub -> Microsoft\n'
  + '   - Luxury: Louis Vuitton -> LVMH, Fendi -> LVMH, Dior -> LVMH, Gucci -> Kering, Balenciaga -> Kering\n'
  + '   - Industrial: Porsche -> Volkswagen Group\n'
  + '   - If you have to think twice about whether it is universally known, it is NOT universally known.\n\n'
  + 'MEDIUM CONFIDENCE — likely but not universally famous:\n\n'
  + '4. LIKELY DIVISION/BRAND of a parent company:\n'
  + '   - The name strongly suggests it is a division (e.g., "Keepmoat Regeneration" -> Keepmoat)\n'
  + '   - The parent relationship is plausible but not common knowledge\n'
  + '   - You are fairly confident but it is not a household-name relationship\n\n'
  + 'ALWAYS INDEPENDENT — never classify these as subsidiaries:\n'
  + '- Government agencies, ministries, public authorities\n'
  + '- Universities, university hospitals, research institutes\n'
  + '- Non-profits, associations, foundations (e.V., Stiftung, Verband, Kuratorium)\n'
  + '- Airlines (e.g., Ita Airways, Vueling, Eurowings) — even if owned by a group\n'
  + '- Companies with geography in their BRAND NAME (e.g., Berlin Brands Group, Heidelberg Engineering, Swiss Life)\n'
  + '- Consumer email/cloud domains (icloud.com, me.com, gmail.com, outlook.com) — these are NOT company domains\n'
  + '- When the "parent" would be THE SAME ENTITY (e.g., "Kotak Mahindra" is NOT a subsidiary of "Kotak Mahindra")\n'
  + '- When in doubt -> INDEPENDENT. A wrong "independent" is harmless. A wrong "subsidiary" causes incorrect data.\n\n'
  + 'COMPANIES TO CLASSIFY:\n'
  + companies.map(c => {
    let line = '[' + c.index + '] "' + c.name + '" (domain: ' + (c.domain || 'none') + ')';
    return line;
  }).join('\n')
  + '\n\nNOTE: Only company name and domain are provided. Do NOT use domain patterns (TLDs, subdomains) as evidence of subsidiary relationships. Classification is based purely on company name and your knowledge of well-known corporate structures.\n\n'
  + 'Return ONLY a valid JSON array (no markdown, no explanation):\n'
  + '[{"index": 0, "isSubsidiary": true, "confidence": "high", "parentCompany": "KPMG", "parentDomain": "kpmg.com"}]\n\n'
  + 'Rules:\n'
  + '- For independent companies: isSubsidiary=false, confidence=null, parentCompany=null, parentDomain=null\n'
  + '- For high-confidence subsidiaries: isSubsidiary=true, confidence="high", parentCompany=parent name, parentDomain=parent primary domain\n'
  + '- For medium-confidence subsidiaries: isSubsidiary=true, confidence="medium", parentCompany=parent name, parentDomain=parent primary domain\n'
  + '- When unsure, ALWAYS default to independent (isSubsidiary=false)';

const companyData = items.map(item => item.json);

return [{
  json: {
    prompt,
    companyData: JSON.stringify(companyData),
    companyCount: companies.length
  }
}];

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contents: [{ parts: [{ text: $json.prompt }] }], generationConfig: { temperature: 0.2 } }) }}
